responses: Hello! I can help with résumé screening, skills, experience, and interview preparation.

patterns: how can I improve my resume
what should I add to my resume
responses: Tailor your résumé to the job description. Use clear sections, list relevant skills, and write experience bullets with measurable outcomes such as time saved, revenue improved, or projects delivered.

patterns: how do I list technical skills
responses: Include skills that are genuinely relevant to the role. Group technical skills clearly, then support them with evidence in projects or experience. Include communication, teamwork, and problem-solving only when you can demonstrate them.

patterns: how do I write work experience
responses: Use internships, academic projects, freelance work, volunteering, or coursework as experience. For each item, state the action, tools used, and result: for example, ‘Built a Python dashboard that reduced report preparation time.’

patterns: how do I write education section
responses: List your highest or most relevant qualification first. Include degree, institution, graduation year, and job-relevant certifications. Add grades only when they strengthen your application or are requested.

patterns: how do I prepare for interview
how should I answer interview questions
responses: Review the job description, prepare examples using the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result), and be ready to explain every skill and project listed on your résumé honestly.

Recruiters and ATS systems usually look for job-relevant qualifications, clear job titles, relevant keywords, and evidence of impact. Use simple formatting and tailor your résumé to each role, but never add skills you do not have.
Good luck with your application! Keep your résumé accurate, focused, and tailored to the role.
